stemHtml: What is correct regarding the general description of turbulence?

options.a: It is defined as the horizontal (NOT vertical) motion of air around obstacles on the surface, and below and between the surfaces of two air masses.
options.b: It is defined as irregular and instantaneous motions of air, which can be made up of a number of small eddies that travel in the general air current.
options.c: It is defined as the vertical (NOT horizontal) motion of air up and down that is caused by instability, particularly above a warm surface below.
options.d: It is defined as a rapid change of wind velocity (speed and /or direction) above the prevailing strength, with a duration of less than one minute.
